name: smith standing behind head military press
instructions: Adjust the seat height of the smith machine so that the bar is at shoulder level.
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lift the bar off the rack and step back, maintaining a stable stance.
Position the bar behind your head, resting on your upper traps.
Keep your core engaged and your chest lifted throughout the exercise.
Press the bar overhead by extending your arms, fully straightening them.
Pause briefly at the top of the movement, then slowly lower the bar back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

Adjust the seat height of the smith machine so that the bar is at shoulder level. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent. Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Lift the bar off the rack and step back, maintaining a stable stance. Position the bar behind your head, resting on your upper traps. Keep your core engaged and your chest lifted throughout the exercise. Press the bar overhead by extending your arms, fully straightening them. Pause briefly at the top of the movement, then slowly lower the bar back down to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Favor symmetrical loading and supported variations if fatigue changes your posture.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Avoid loaded spinal flexion and excessive hinge depth. Brace core and use supported positions.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
